title: Knowledge Entry
description:
A piece of knowledge available to agents. Knowledge is stratified by layer, determining how it is accessed (always in prompt, retrieved via tool, etc.). Supports the menu+consult pattern with summary (menu) and details (consult).

STUDIO DOGMA vs REFERENCE MATERIAL: Use structured/file_ref for Studio Dogma (internal rules, brand voice, project-specific facts). Use 'corpus' type for Reference Material (external genre guides, general craft knowledge) - this points to a RAG-searchable store without polluting the agent's context menu.

NOTE: The 'inline' content type has been deprecated in favor of 'structured'. All must_know entries should use structured content with semantic types (rule, contract, criterion, heuristic, definition, procedure, warning).

id.description: Unique identifier for this knowledge entry

layer.description: Which knowledge layer this belongs to

summary.description: Brief summary for the 'menu' - shown in system prompt for must_know, or as search result preview. Keep concise for context efficiency.

concise_summary.description: Menu display for small models. Simpler than summary, removes nuances small models can't handle. Empty string means exclude from small model context entirely. If undefined, falls back to 'summary'.

concise_description.description: Full content for small models when entry is consulted. Simpler than normal description, may omit edge cases and conditional logic. Empty string means exclude from small model consult results. If undefined, falls back to normal rendered content.

injection_priority.description: Controls ordering in prompt to address 'Lost in the Middle' attention issues. Critical items appear at start AND end of prompt (sandwich pattern). High items near start, low items in middle.

keywords.description: Keywords for semantic matching and search. Include synonyms, related terms, and domain-specific vocabulary.

triggers.description: Phrases or patterns that suggest this knowledge should be consulted. E.g., 'character voice', 'dialogue style', 'how to write dialogue'.

discriminators.description: What makes this entry DIFFERENT from similar entries. Helps distinguish between multiple entries with similar summaries.

last_updated.description: When this entry was last updated

type.description: How content is provided. 'structured' = semantic JSON validated against structured-content.schema.json. 'file_ref' = external file (rare, for very large content). 'corpus' = RAG-searchable reference material.

file_path.description: For file_ref type: path to the file containing content. MUST be relative to the studio root directory (e.g., 'knowledge/style-guide.md'). Never use absolute paths.

format.description: Format of the content. Default is 'json' for structured type.

data.description: For structured type: semantic content following structured-content.schema.json. Contains arrays of rules, contracts, criteria, heuristics, definitions, procedures, and warnings.

schema_ref.description: For structured type: schema the data conforms to. Typically 'knowledge/structured-content.schema.json#/$defs/structured_entry'.

corpus_ref.description: For corpus type: defines a RAG-searchable reference collection. The Runtime routes consult queries to vector search over this corpus.

corpus_reference.description: Reference to a corpus of documents for RAG-based retrieval. The 'Reference Shelf' pattern: store general domain knowledge (genre guides, craft books, heuristics) in a cold store, and let agents search it on demand without cluttering their context.

store_ref.description: The store containing the corpus documents. Should typically be a 'cold' store with consumption_guidance='none'.

path_pattern.description: Glob pattern to filter which files in the store are part of this corpus. E.g., 'genres/mystery/**' to limit to mystery-related references.

chunk_size.description: Target chunk size in tokens for RAG indexing

embedding_model.description: Optional: specific embedding model to use for this corpus

index_config.description: Optional configuration for how the corpus is indexed

archetypes.description: Which archetypes this knowledge is relevant to

agents.description: Specific agents this knowledge is for

playbooks.description: Playbooks where this knowledge is relevant

artifact_types.description: Artifact types this knowledge relates to
